title: Depth-First Search, Recursion, Paths, and Backtracking · Language Clinic: Dfs Visit Timing
summary: DFS marks on return, confuses visitation with solution path, fails to undo dead ends, and promises shortest routes. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the dfs visit timing increment in traceable DFS solver with path witness. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Mark a vertex when discovered so cycles and converging paths cannot schedule repeated work.
Define start, goal, passable-neighbor order, visited policy, result, and failure contract before traversal.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-dfs-solver-recursion-3
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-dfs-solver-recursion-3.
# Case 5798: a clean desktop release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: the player cannot rely on color or animation.
# Changed case: change the seed and retain replay identity.
def worked_maze_dfs_solver_recursion_3(path_steps: int = 31, valid_steps: int = 31, explored: int = 42, repeated_vertices: int = 0, recursion_safe: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "gridworked3",
        "topology5798",
        "search3dekrow",
        "eventworked35798",
        "access5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if path_steps < 1 or valid_steps < 0 or explored < path_steps:
        return False, valid_steps, "dfs-count"
    if valid_steps != path_steps:
        return False, valid_steps, "invalid-path-step"
    if repeated_vertices > 0:
        return False, repeated_vertices, "visited-policy"
    if not recursion_safe:
        return False, explored, "recursion-limit"
    assert valid_steps == path_steps
    return True, path_steps, "valid-dfs-route"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-dfs-visit-timing-9sug4s
# Competency: maze-dfs-visit-timing.
# Case 8400: a low-vision player session.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: replace pointer input with a single-action keyboard path.
def evidence_maze_dfs_visit_timing_9sug4s(path_steps: int = 31, valid_steps: int = 31, explored: int = 42, repeated_vertices: int = 0, recursion_safe: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_9sug4s = "9sug4s-s4gus9-8400"
    assert evidence_variant_9sug4s
    evidence_axes_9sug4s = (
        "grid9sug4s",
        "topology8400",
        "searchs4gus9",
        "event9sug4s8400",
        "access84009sug4s",
        "recoverys4gus98400",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_9sug4s)) == 6
    if path_steps < 1 or valid_steps < 0 or explored < path_steps:
        return False, valid_steps, "dfs-count"
    if valid_steps != path_steps:
        return False, valid_steps, "invalid-path-step"
    if repeated_vertices > 0:
        return False, repeated_vertices, "visited-policy"
    if not recursion_safe:
        return False, explored, "recursion-limit"
    assert valid_steps == path_steps
    return True, path_steps, "valid-dfs-route"
